workflow.name: Track student attendance from CSV, email parents, and build an HTML dashboard
workflow.description:
## 🚀 Overview

Automatically monitors daily student attendance from CSV files, identifies absent students, sends parent email alerts via SMTP, calculates risk scores, and generates an interactive HTML dashboard — all on a weekday schedule with no manual work needed.

---

## ⚙️ How it works

1. **Schedule trigger** — runs Monday–Friday at 17:30
2. **Data ingestion** — reads `student_attendance.csv` and filters to today's records only
3. **Absence check** — splits students into Absent / Present branches
4. **Contact merge** — matches absent students with parent data from `student_contacts.csv`
5. **Alert logic** — calculates risk level, attendance %, consecutive streak, and trend (Improving / Stable / Worsening)
6. **Parent email** — sends a colour-coded HTML alert via SMTP for each absent student
7. **Dashboard builder** — generates `dashboard.html` with 5 tabs: Today, Weekly, Monthly, Full History, and At-Risk
8. **Report update** — appends records to `attendance_report.csv` for historical tracking

---

## 🛠 Setup steps

1. Upload `student_attendance.csv` to the n8n files folder
2. Upload `student_contacts.csv` to the n8n files folder
3. Create an empty `attendance_report.csv` in the n8n files folder
4. Add `smtp_user` in **Settings → Variables**
5. Configure SMTP credentials in the **Send Email** node
6. Adjust the cron schedule if needed (default: 17:30 Mon–Fri)

---

## 🔧 Required CSV formats

**student_attendance.csv**

| StudentID | Date | Status | Class | Subject | Teacher |
|-----------|------|--------|-------|---------|---------|
| S101 | 12-01-2026 | Absent | Grade 6 | Math | Mr Singh |

**student_contacts.csv**

| StudentID | ParentName | Email | Phone |
|-----------|------------|-------|-------|
| S101 | Anita Kumar | parent@email.com | +919999999999 |

# 📊 Dashboard Preview
![Dashboard Preview 1](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tejasv694/n8n-template-images/refs/heads/main/Screenshot%202026-03-22%20105318.png)

![ Dashboard Preview 2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tejasv694/n8n-template-images/refs/heads/main/Screenshot%202026-03-22%20105333.png)

workflow.name: Track student attendance from CSV, alert parents by email, and generate an HTML dashboard

parameters.content:
## How it works

1. Runs every weekday at 17:30 (Mon–Fri)
2. Reads `student_attendance.csv` and filters to today's records only
3. Splits students into Absent / Present branches
4. Fetches parent contacts from `student_contacts.csv` and merges the data
5. Calculates risk level, attendance %, consecutive streak, and trend
6. Sends a colour-coded HTML email alert to parents of absent students via SMTP
7. Builds and saves an interactive `dashboard.html` with 5 tabs: Today, Weekly, Monthly, Full History, At-Risk
8. Appends today's records to `attendance_report.csv` for historical tracking

## Setup steps

1. Upload `student_attendance.csv` to the n8n files folder
2. Upload `student_contacts.csv` to the n8n files folder
3. Create an empty `attendance_report.csv` in the n8n files folder
4. Add `smtp_user` in n8n → Settings → Variables
5. Configure SMTP credentials in the **Send Email** node
6. Adjust the cron schedule if needed (default: 17:30 Mon–Fri)

## Trigger & data ingestion

Schedule trigger reads attendance CSV and filters to today's records only.

## Absence routing & contact merge

Splits absent vs present students, fetches parent contacts, and merges records.

## Alert logic

Calculates risk level, attendance %, streak, and trend per student.

## Parent notifications

Generates and sends a colour-coded HTML email alert to parents via SMTP.

## No absences branch

Skips email and dashboard when all students are present today.

nodes.name: Build Visual Report and Update Attendance File

parameters.content:
## Dashboard & report update

Builds interactive HTML dashboard and appends today's records to attendance history CSV.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
